vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but shows significant degradation. P3 (Pothos with rabbit) remains the anchor. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are in the black pot, but show severe decline.

plant_audit.P1: Stable, though growth is minimal. Soil appears moist.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Severe chlorosis and necrosis. The specimens are essentially desiccated remnants.
plant_audit.P3: The Pothos leaf is showing signs of yellowing and potential senescence, though the structural integrity of the pot remains.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark across all pots; no visible cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Debris (white perlite/egg fragments) present in P3. No fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Gradual yellowing of the Pothos leaf in P3. P2/P4 show no recovery.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: Lighting conditions fluctuated (T-2 was underexposed). The Pothos leaf in P3 has maintained its position but shows persistent chlorosis.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of 'Stagnant Decline'. P2 and P4 are non-viable. P3 is under stress (yellowing leaf). P1 is the only specimen showing baseline stability.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a yellowing, dying leaf in P3 suggests potential overwatering or root-zone stress, given the dark, damp soil appearance.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a high-stress environment. While the rabbit anchor remains, the biological occupants are failing. The yellowing of the Pothos leaf in P3 is a critical indicator of poor health, likely linked to the moisture levels observed in the soil. P2 and P4 are beyond recovery.
